question_text: A student builds an electromagnet using a coil of wire wrapped around an iron core connected to a battery. Which change would make the electromagnet stronger?

options.label: A
options.text: Decreasing the number of turns in the coil

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the current flowing through the wire

options.label: C
options.text: Using a plastic core instead of an iron core

options.label: D
options.text: Disconnecting the battery so there is no current

explanation: The strength of an electromagnet depends on the current flowing through it and the number of turns in the coil. Increasing the current makes the magnetic field stronger. Using a plastic core or reducing turns would weaken it, and disconnecting the battery would stop the electromagnet working entirely.
